ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-03-2025 Continued To Nov-25-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-05-2025 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT EMPLOYMENT DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant the Employment Development Department of the State of California (EDD)'s demurrer to plaintiff Hiaqing Qiu's Second Amended Complaint (SAC) is sustained without leave to amend.

This demurrer comes after the court sustained EDD's previous demurrer to the first amended complaint with leave to amend. The court granted plaintiff leave to amend on limited terms: "If he can amend in good faith to plead his compliance with the claims presentation and exhaustion requirements before bringing suit, he has leave to do so. His amendment must also cure the deficiency that he does not identify a statutory basis permitting him to recover damages from EDD."

On April 16, 2025, Qiu filed a document captioned "Second Amended Complaint." Although captioned as a complaint, the document is not a pleading but instead argument that the court's previous demurrer order was incorrect. On May 29, 2025, after EDD filed its demurrer, Qiu filed a document captioned "Second Amended Complaint Part 2." The court has considered both documents along with Qiu's First Amended Complaint together to see whether Qiu has pleaded facts that, if true, would entitle him to relief.

Qiu has not cured the defects of the prior pleading. EDD is liable only as provided by statute. (Gov. Code 815(a).) Qiu identifies no statute under which he can recover in a civil action apart from through exhaustion of the administrative appeals process and then filing of a writ petition. Moreover, the Government Tort Claims Act requires a claimant to present a government claim before suing for money or damages. (Gov. Code 810.) Plaintiff does not plead that he submitted a government claim to EDD. Finally, Qiu does not plead or offer to plead that he exhausted his administrative remedies before filing this lawsuit.

Because the court identified the deficiencies in the prior pleading and Qiu has been unable to overcome them, it appears to this court that it would be futile to allow any further amendment. The court sustains the demurrer without leave to amend.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Improperly Filed "Second Amended Complaint Part Two" Or, In The Alternative, Reply In Support Of Motion To Dismiss Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-05-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT EMPLOYMENT DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT's Motion To Strike Improperly Filed "Second Amended Complaint Part Two" Or, In The Alternative, Reply In Support Of Motion To Dismiss Second Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant the Employment Development Department of the State of California (EDD)'s motion to strike the document captioned "Second Amended Complaint Part Two" is granted.

Plaintiff Hiaqing Qiu was granted leave to amend the complaint and did so by a filing dated April 16, 2025. The document that is the subject of this motion, "SAC Part Two," was filed on May 29, 2025. Construed as a pleading, SAC Part Two is stricken because it was filed without leave of the court. (See Code Civ. Proc. 472(a) [party may file an amended pleading once only at times specified by statute].)

The court has also construed SAC Part Two as an opposition to EDD's demurrer and as an offer of what Qiu could plead if given further leave to amend. As discussed in the companion order on EDD's demurrer, Qiu has not identified facts that could be pled that would survive demurrer.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-06-2025 Continued To May-06-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-17-2025 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT EMPLOYMENT DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Hiaqing Qiu filed a First Amended Complaint (FAC) alleging that he should have received certain COVID-related benefits from defendant Employment Development Department, including unemployment benefits and rental assistance. The complaint also contains exhibits showing Qiu's applications for these benefits, his supporting documentation, and his appeals. Defendant EDD now demurs to the FAC in its entirety. Plaintiff opposes (in an overlength filing. See Rule of Court 3.1113(d). The court admonishes Plaintiff to adhere to the page limits of the rule in the future; if Plaintiff does not, the court may strike Plaintiff's brief or strike all pages past the limit.)

The court sustains the demurrer with leave to amend. EDD is liable only as provided by statute. (Gov. Code 815(a).) Qiu identifies no statute under which he can recover. Moreover, the Government Tort Claims Act requires a claimant to present a government claim before suing for money or damages. (Gov. Code 810.) Plaintiff does not plead that he submitted a government claim to EDD or that he exhausted his administrative remedies prior to bringing suit.

Plaintiff is granted leave to amend only as follows. If he can amend in good faith to plead his compliance with the claims presentation and exhaustion requirements before bringing suit, he has leave to do so. His amendment must also cure the deficiency that he does not identify a statutory basis permitting him to recover damages from EDD. Plaintiff has 30 days from entry of this order to amend.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
